steps.title: Use the vertex location
steps.content:
All four models open upward and have leading coefficient $2$. The vertex has $x$-coordinate $-\frac{b}{2a}$. Choices with $b=-11$ have
$$x=\frac{11}{4}=2.75$$
Choices with $b=-5$ have
$$x=\frac{5}{4}=1.25$$
The scatterplot minimum is near $x=3$, so keep the models with $b=-11$.

steps.title: Test a plotted point
steps.content:
A visible point on the scatterplot is near $(5,15)$. Test the two remaining models.
$$2(5)^2-11(5)-20=50-55-20=-25$$
$$2(5)^2-11(5)+20=50-55+20=15$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: The model $y=2x^2-11x+20$ has the right vertex location and matches the visible point near $(5,15)$. The answer is B.
